title: T-009 — to_state_machine_v1 IN_TRANSIT state extension + V-MS-09/12 guards (§8.2, §11.3)
prompt:
# T-009 — to_state_machine_v1 IN_TRANSIT extension + V-MS-09/12 guards

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §8.2 to_state_machine_v1 extension, §11.3 V-MS-09 (cross-site requires IN_TRANSIT), V-MS-12 (same-site skip IN_TRANSIT)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Extend the existing `to_state_machine_v1` rule (owned by 05-WH; rule body in rule_definitions per 02-SET §7.8) to include an `IN_TRANSIT` state. Cross-site TOs (from_site_id != to_site_id) MUST transition via IN_TRANSIT; same-site TOs MUST NOT (V-MS-12 — keep direct shipped→received path).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0047_to_state_machine_in_transit.sql`:
   - UPDATE rule_definitions SET body = jsonb_set(...) WHERE name='to_state_machine_v1' — add IN_TRANSIT state node + transitions {draft→planned→shipped→IN_TRANSIT→received→closed} for cross-site, {draft→planned→shipped→received→closed} for same-site.
   - Version bump to v2; previous v1 retained for replay safety.
2. New file `packages/domain/src/transfer-orders/state-machine.ts` exports `nextAllowedStates(currentState, isCrossSite)` and `canTransition(from, to, isCrossSite)` reading the rule body.
3. Guard middleware `packages/domain/src/transfer-orders/transition-guard.ts` — Server Action wrapper that rejects illegal transitions with typed `InvalidStateTransition` error including reason.
4. Tests: matrix of all transitions for both cross-site and same-site paths; V-MS-09 violation (cross-site shipped → received directly) rejected; V-MS-12 violation (same-site shipped → IN_TRANSIT) rejected.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0047_to_state_machine_in_transit.sql [create]
- packages/domain/src/transfer-orders/state-machine.ts [create]
- packages/domain/src/transfer-orders/transition-guard.ts [create]
- packages/domain/__tests__/transfer-orders/state-machine.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given rule v2 is applied, when state-machine.ts is loaded, then nextAllowedStates('shipped', isCrossSite=true) === ['IN_TRANSIT'] and nextAllowedStates('shipped', isCrossSite=false) === ['received'].
2. Given a cross-site TO in state='shipped', when transition-guard receives action 'received', then InvalidStateTransition is thrown with reason 'V-MS-09: cross-site TO must pass through IN_TRANSIT'.
3. Given a same-site TO in state='shipped', when transition-guard receives action 'IN_TRANSIT', then InvalidStateTransition is thrown with reason 'V-MS-12: same-site TO must skip IN_TRANSIT'.
4. Given the rule body is queried, when version is inspected, then v2 exists and v1 is retained (for replay) — v2 has IN_TRANSIT node, v1 does not.
5. Given a cross-site TO in state='IN_TRANSIT', when transition-guard receives 'received', then transition is allowed and returns next_state='received'.

## Test strategy
- RED: assert all 6 state machine transitions in matrix form (cross-site and same-site) before state-machine.ts exists.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/domain test transfer-orders/state-machine'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not destroy v1 of the rule — append v2; replay/audit of historical TOs needs v1.
- Do not encode transitions in TypeScript constants — read from rule_definitions so DSL stays canonical (02-SET §7.8 source of truth).
- Do not allow IN_TRANSIT for same-site TOs — V-MS-12 explicit guard.
- Do not skip approval gate enforcement at this layer — T-010 owns approval gates; this task only owns state transitions.
- Do not couple state machine to a specific TO database adapter — pure domain function.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: v1 of the rule is owned by 05-WH baseline; this task extends it as v2.
Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext composes on top of T-125; all site-scoped tasks must carry this cross-module dep.

acceptance_criteria: Given rule v2 is applied, when state-machine.ts loaded, then nextAllowedStates('shipped', isCrossSite=true) === ['IN_TRANSIT'] and nextAllowedStates('shipped', isCrossSite=false) === ['received'].
Given rule body queried, when version inspected, then v2 exists with IN_TRANSIT node; v1 retained.

Given cross-site TO state='shipped', when transition-guard receives 'received', then InvalidStateTransition thrown with reason 'V-MS-09: cross-site TO must pass through IN_TRANSIT'; AND given same-site TO state='shipped' when transition-guard receives 'IN_TRANSIT' then InvalidStateTransition thrown (same-site cross-flow guard).
Given cross-site TO state='IN_TRANSIT', when transition-guard receives 'received', then transition allowed and next_state='received'.
test_strategy: RED: assert all 6 transitions in matrix (cross-site + same-site) before state-machine.ts exists.

Do not hardcode transitions in TS — read rule_definitions (02-SET §7.8 canonical).
Do not allow IN_TRANSIT for same-site (V-MS-12).
Do not enforce approval gates here (T-010 owns).
